title: Artifact for Paper (Transforming Analysis Tools to Web Services via OSLC)
author: Ondřej Vašíček
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This artifact was created for TACAS 22 artifact evaluation and is designed to work in the TACAS 22 Artifact Evaluation VM - Ubuntu 20.04 LTS (10.5281/zenodo.5562597). To use this artifact unzip the archive in the "~/" directory and then follow the README located in the unzipped archive. 

   

 The paper "Transforming Analysis Tools to Web Services via OSLC" introduced Unite, an universal analysis adapter based on the OSLC standard, which allows analysis tools to be transformed into web services. 

 The artifact contains Unite configured with a number of analysis tools to demonstrate that all of the analysis tools can be executed through Unite. The abstract features two videos one of which covers the whole artifact demonstration process along with a detailed readme which explains all demo examples in detail. All the demonstration steps are perform using Postman, a REST client which we used to interact with Unite's REST API. The artifact also demonstrates how to create configuration for a new analysis tool to be able to use it through Unite. 

 A few steps in the artifact demonstration require internet access for the VM. These steps are entirely optional and the reviewer can choose to skip them if they do not want to enable internet connection. The steps include executing analysis using ANaConDA because it is only available remotely due to not working on Ubuntu 20, and two ways of transferring SUT source files (download from a URL, and cloning a Git repository). 

   

 Links 

 
	 Unite Git repository - https://pajda.fit.vutbr.cz/verifit/unite 
	 ANaConDA - https://pajda.fit.vutbr.cz/anaconda/anaconda 
	 Postman - https://www.postman.com/product/what-is-postman/ 
	 Postman Terms of Service - https://www.postman.com/legal/terms/
